title: The Foolish Lion and the Clever Rabbit

content.text: **The Foolish Lion and the Clever Rabbit**  Long ago, in a sun-dappled forest where the trees stretched tall and the rivers sparkled like silver ribbons, there lived a mighty lion named Rajah.
His golden mane shimmered in the sunlight, and his roar could shake the leaves from the branches.
Rajah was the king of the jungle, and all the animals bowed to him—except one.
Every day, Rajah would demand a tribute. "Bring me food!" he would roar.
"Or I shall eat *you* instead!" Terrified, the animals would take turns bringing him fruits, nuts, or even a plump deer.
But one day, the animals grew tired of living in fear.
They huddled together under the banyan tree, whispering nervously.
"We cannot keep doing this," said the wise old tortoise, his voice slow and steady.
"Soon, there will be no animals left to feed him!"  The clever monkey scratched his chin.
"We need a plan. A way to outsmart Rajah without fighting him."  Just then, a tiny rabbit hopped forward.
His ears twitched, and his nose wiggled. "I… I can do it," he squeaked. The other animals gasped.
A rabbit? Against the mighty lion? "You?" scoffed the deer.
"You’re barely bigger than his paw!"  But the rabbit stood tall.
"Size doesn’t matter when you have a clever mind."  The next day, the rabbit hopped slowly toward Rajah’s den, dragging his feet as if he carried the weight of the world.
Rajah lounged on a rock, licking his lips. "Where is my food?" he growled. The rabbit bowed low.
"Oh, great king! I am so sorry to keep you waiting.
But… but there was a problem."  Rajah’s ears perked up.
"What problem?"  "There was *another* lion in the forest," the rabbit said, trembling. "A bigger, fiercer lion!
He said *he* was the true king, and he chased me away!"  Rajah’s eyes blazed with fury. "Another lion?
In *my* forest?
Show me this imposter at once!"  The rabbit led Rajah deep into the jungle, past tangled vines and whispering bushes, until they reached a deep, dark well.
The water inside shimmered like a mirror. "There he is!" the rabbit cried, pointing.
"He’s hiding in the well!"  Rajah leaned over the edge, his reflection staring back at him—golden mane, fierce eyes, just like his own.
"How dare he!" Rajah roared.
"I’ll teach him a lesson!" And with that, he leaped into the well, thinking he was attacking his rival.
*Splash!*  The water swallowed him whole. Rajah thrashed and roared, but the well was too deep.
No matter how hard he tried, he could not climb out.
The rabbit peeked over the edge, his heart pounding. "Goodbye, foolish king," he whispered.
Then he turned and ran back to the forest, where the animals cheered.
From that day on, the animals lived in peace.
Rajah, trapped in the well, learned his lesson—pride and cruelty only lead to downfall. And the clever rabbit?
Well, he became a hero, proving that even the smallest creature can outsmart the mightiest foe.
**Moral:** *Wisdom and cleverness can defeat even the strongest enemy.*
